title: Information-Theoretic Reward Decomposition for Generalizable RLHF

introduction.topic: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) for Large Language Models (LLMs) alignment
key_points: Reward learning plays a pivotal role in RLHF methods
Standard reward training doesn't guarantee sufficient generalization capability
Reward models often focus on distinguishing chosen vs. rejected responses without considering the corresponding prompt
Uses Bradley-Terry (BT) model as an example to illustrate the issue

findings: Reward models can maintain reward gaps even when prompts are replaced with other prompts
Responses can dominate the reward gap, leading to poor generalization
Identifies two components of reward value: 'prompt-free reward' and 'prompt-related reward'
results.proposed_solution: A novel method of decomposition from an information-theoretic perspective

method.problem: Failure case where the reward gap overly depends on responses
method.description: Solid lines represent prompt-response pairs used in training, while dashed lines represent non-corresponding pairs
method.issue: The reward gap generalizes poorly to novel prompt-response pairs
